items.scenario: You are a second-year student who missed the registration window for a required seminar that fills up quickly. The department secretary, who manages course enrollment, can add you from the waitlist.
items.direction: Write an email to the department secretary. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you missed the registration window
Ask whether a waitlist spot is still available
Describe how this seminar fits your degree plan
items.subject: Waitlist request for the required seminar

items.scenario: You recently received a midterm grade that seems lower than the score you calculated from the graded sections. Your professor holds office hours twice a week and reviews grading questions then.
items.direction: Write an email to your professor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the discrepancy you noticed in the grade
Ask how the midterm total was calculated
Request a time to review your exam during office hours
items.subject: Question about my midterm grade

items.scenario: You signed up for a weekend pottery workshop at the campus arts center, but the confirmation email lists a different date than the one posted online. The coordinator handles all class scheduling.
items.direction: Write an email to the workshop coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the date conflict between the email and the website
Ask which date is correct
Tell the coordinator which session you originally intended to join
items.subject: Confirming my pottery workshop date

items.scenario: You are a member of the campus film club that booked the main auditorium for a screening next Friday. The events office manages the venue and just notified you the projector will be serviced that day.
items.direction: Write an email to the events office staff member. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain how the projector servicing affects your screening
Ask whether a portable projector can be provided
Suggest moving the screening to the smaller theater if needed
items.subject: Projector availability for Friday's screening

items.scenario: You completed a summer marketing internship last month and the supervisor mentioned a possible part-time role for the fall. You have not heard back and the semester starts soon.
items.direction: Write an email to your former internship supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank the supervisor for the internship experience
Ask whether the part-time role is still open
Tell the supervisor your availability for the fall term
items.subject: Following up on the fall part-time role

items.scenario: You live in a campus apartment where the laundry room has had one broken washing machine for over two weeks. The residence manager handles maintenance requests for the building.
items.direction: Write an email to the residence manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how long the washer has been out of service
Ask when a repair is scheduled
Suggest posting a notice so residents can plan around it
items.subject: Broken washer in the laundry room

items.scenario: A friend invited you to co-host a board game night, but you already promised to help your roommate move that same evening. You want to stay involved without dropping either commitment.
items.direction: Write an email to your friend. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the scheduling conflict you ran into
Ask whether the game night could start later
Suggest a backup plan in case the time cannot change
items.subject: About the board game night this weekend

items.scenario: You are applying for a competitive study-abroad program and need a recommendation letter. Your academic advisor knows your coursework well and the application deadline is in three weeks.
items.direction: Write an email to your academic advisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain which program you are applying to
Ask whether the advisor can write a recommendation
Tell the advisor the deadline and how to submit it
items.subject: Recommendation letter for study-abroad application
